prompt: Build v6 of the Watchtower board: add a THIRD tab 'Map' — a LIVE OPERATIONAL view of the running system (this is live status, NOT an architecture diagram — architecture lives in docs, not the app). The board is experiments/watchtower-board/server.mjs; tabs are currently Floor / Lanes — make them [Floor] [Lanes] [Map]. The Map view shows three rows: (A) APPS — status dots for the constellation: Watchtower (this server, always 'up'), Switchboard (health-check http://127.0.0.1:5099/health), AngelEye (health-check http://127.0.0.1:5051/api/health), AppyCtrl ('unknown — not wired'). green=up, red=down, grey=unknown. (B) FLOOR — the engine pipeline counts: queue / running / done / failed (failed = count of experiments/watchtower-engine/failed/*.json). (C) WATCHERS — static info labels (the board can't introspect Marshall's Monitors): 'reaper → watches done/, closes finished windows' and 'retry → watches failed/, re-queues (backoff, max 3)'. STEPS: (1) Add endpoint GET /api/health-map that server-side fetches the two health URLs with a SHORT timeout (~2s, AbortController) inside try/catch so it NEVER crashes → returns JSON {watchtower:'up', switchboard:'up'|'down', angeleye:'up'|'down', appyctrl:'unknown'}. (2) Reuse /api/state for the floor counts (add `failed` count there if missing, additively). (3) Add the Map tab + view that polls /api/health-map + /api/state every ~3s (pause the others while Map is shown, matching how Floor/Lanes already pause); reuse the existing CSS vars/cards; render the three rows clean (status dots, count pills, watcher labels). (4) HARD CONSTRAINTS: additive only — do NOT change Floor, Lanes, /api/concepts, /api/converge, or the staleness logic; pure Node + vanilla JS, no deps; all server-side health checks fail safe. (5) TEST without disturbing :7430: factor the health-map builder so it can be unit-checked with mocked fetch results (or a node --check + a small pure-function self-check); do NOT start/kill servers. (6) Proof to experiments/watchtower-engine/proof/board-v6-map.md (max 6 lines). Then complete run-next-workflow bookkeeping.
